strategy_summary: The binary is a 64-bit ELF with NX enabled and no PIE. The vulnerable function reads up to 512 bytes into a 128-byte buffer, causing a stack overflow. We can use this to overwrite the return address. Since there is no RELRO, we can overwrite the GOT entries. We will leak a libc address (e.g., system) and then use it to calculate the base address of libc. Finally, we will call system('/bin/sh') to get a shell.
